meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client work. Learn how to implement scalable SaaS pricing models to finish projects faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often scope creep disguised as 'perfecting' features. By implementing a rigid, tiered SaaS pricing model early, you force yourself to define clear product boundaries. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing architecture and Claude Code to automate the deployment of these constraints.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished dashboard, the client is pinging you for an ETA, and the feature list keeps expanding like a black hole. The 'can't finish' syndrome isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of boundaries. When you don't define the value exchange, you end up building a playground instead of a product.

The secret to shipping isn't working harder—it's weaponizing your pricing model. By treating your SaaS pricing structure as your project roadmap, you move from 'infinite developer' to 'strategic architect.' Let's turn that unfinished repo into a production-ready asset.

steps.title: Define Tiered Boundaries
steps.description: Map your features to three distinct pricing tiers. This prevents scope creep by strictly defining what is 'Basic' vs 'Pro'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature-to-tier mapping schema for a SaaS billing system. Define 3 tiers: Starter, Growth, Enterprise. Ensure the schema supports dynamic feature flagging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a feature_flags.json config based on the antigravity schema'

steps.title: Scaffold the Billing Logic
steps.description: Build the core logic that checks user tier status before executing protected functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a middleware pattern in TypeScript that validates user subscription status against the feature_flags.json file before executing controller methods.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the auth middleware using the generated logic'

steps.title: Automate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Use boilerplate code to connect your tiers to actual payment processing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler that updates user subscription status in our database based on session completion events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create the stripe webhook route and test it locally'

steps.title: Implement Usage Metering
steps.description: Track usage to enforce limits, which creates natural 'upsell' points and prevents resource exhaustion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an event-driven usage tracker that logs API calls per user and triggers a limit-exceeded exception when the tier threshold is reached.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add a redis-based counter for api usage tracking'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push the billing infrastructure live to lock in your project scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates all environment variables and initializes the production database schema for our new billing system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run deployment validation and push to main'

pro_tips: Always price your lowest tier to cover your infrastructure costs plus a 20% margin to avoid burnout.
Use 'Feature Gating' as a development tool: if a feature isn't in a tier, don't build it yet.
Implement 'soft limits' that send an automated email to the user before a hard block occurs.
Treat your pricing page as the primary UI/UX component; it dictates the entire user journey.
Never build custom billing logic; always leverage standard Stripe subscription objects to reduce maintenance overhead.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, iterate on your onboarding flow to ensure users hit the 'Aha!' moment before they reach their usage limits.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start shipping for value. Leverage these tools to regain control of your development lifecycle and finally close those open tickets. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
